reviews.review_description: I like the idea of this game a lot but it's very underwhelming in its current state. Been a couple of years since the last update so could be abandonware. I love the tactical table, but it's a single player game without Ai companions so what's the point of it being there? Clearing a map solo is stressful and panic inducing. This game needs a coop option and/or Ai bots as teamates to be enjoyable. Why that isn't a part of this game baffles me. No melee attack with guns or hands is a big miss. Not a lot of gun options and no character customization either. Could be a very good game with a little more love from the development team.

This game has really good graphics especially for such an early state and the gameplay is very smooth on my part. I know there’s a big update coming out and I really can’t wait this game 
 is really good for its state and I think it has a lot of potential. If you can figure out how to add multiplayer this game I feel like would be known more and gain more players but so far the game is amazing!

reviews.review_title: Co-op would be great for this game
reviews.review_description: This game is great it just gets boring alone so if it is possible to add co-op it would perfect it. You should also make it so you have to hold down grip button to keep gun in hand. Other than that great game.

Very solid CQB game, real AI targets and paper targets for the mix of perfect training. 

-The AI are pretty solid but could maybe have less health.
-All guns have functionable safety but glock which is sort of odd
-Flashbang throwing is very weird, also getting flashed through walls can occur

Besides these issues this game would get a 5/5. I also think multiplayer later down the road would be awesome.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback and review, I have just released an update that fixes the flash bug and lowered the Ai health. Glocks do not have a traditional safety selector like other firearms, this is intentional for realism. If you mean a slide stop then that is on the list.

reviews.review_description:
This is an excellent tactical game. I love the detail in the weapon models and the collision physics feel like some of the best I’ve experienced this far in VR.

Although the title is effectively a clear house with either live or soft targets, the various layout scenarios, difficulty options and weapon offering really make this game stand out. I love being able to pull a pin out with your teeth it’s such a small thing, but fits so perfectly as a mechanic in the game that it gets me buzzing every time I do it.

For me, I do have a couple of suggestions that I think would help make this game a lot more enjoyable.

1. some form of physical gunstock calibrator would be much appreciated. I use a physical stock in every VRFPS game and although I still use it in TRF, the way the rifle sits is just way off and doesn’t fit with my stock.

2. A way of changing the angle for the pistol so that it feels a little more natural in the hand

3. A few more audio queues and more overall ambience sounds. Maybe even some audio lines from the hostiles and civilians.

developer_response.body: I appreciate your review and suggestions Yorick, I have made notes of them.

reviews.review_description: It’s not completed yet but what’s there so far is really good. This will likely become a best seller one day soon. Get in before the price goes up
It's a really cool game, I just wish the movement was different.  I just can't get used to this movement style.  I'm trying.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review, You can change the movement style in the in-game settings tablet, you can switch between “Controller Direction” and “Head Direction” and you also pick if you want a snap or smooth turn. The first video in the tutorial room shows you how to access and use the settings tablet.

reviews.review_title: It's good, but needs a lot of work.
reviews.review_description:
I love it, but it does get boring after awhile. my suggestions would be 
- New CQB map
- More Weapons and Attachments 
- Fullbody Arms
- Polishness - It needs a lot more handling, but I believe.

developer_response.body: I am happy to hear you are loving the game, thank you for your review and suggestions.

reviews.review_description: Any news about this promettor game ?

If you are into tactical breaching games this is for you. The game just got put on app lab so it’s not completely finished but it’s really fun
Ideas:
-enemies will get back up if not shot enough
-fake hostages
(This game kinda reminds me of ready or not)
-maybe you can add some maps like houses or decorated rooms
-arms
-more guns
The game is really good for 13$

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and the ideas.

reviews.review_description:
T
he game is good but multiplayer would make it better you should add multiplayer and your game will be better being with a squad duo trio sounds great/better people would play your game more and lots of people would buy it please add multiplayer Thank you

I do have suggestions though to make this even better and that it of course multiplayer. Multiplayer would help make it more realistic in breaching in with a squad. Another suggestion is possibly night missions, this would also make it more realistic in a way that it could also add night vision. Another suggestion is customization extension, this would like the player pick through a variety of sights, lasers, flashlights, mag mods, suppressors, and more. Commands/negotiation with enemies, this would allow you to command enemies to drop the weapon or put there hands up and more but to also to make the player having to make a decision where the enemy might be tricking the player or actually comply with the player into arresting the enemy. Player customization, if you are adding multiplayer then this would also help so then people don't have to look at usernames to know who is who. Blood settings, this would allow the player to customize the blood into no blood or realistic blood.

A decent game for $13 plus tax. 
Some tips: 
A setting to change the angle while holding the glock, and maybe a higher resolution model.

Gripping a gun, hold and release would be a nice option. also the gun is a little close to the magazines on the chest.

Gripping is kinda slow, there is a slight delay when gripping to when the visuals in game grip.

Maybe a dark mode and add flashlights and lasers attachments.

More guns. 

I like the game, I know this is an indie game, so any update is appreciated. I suggest the developer look at VR Practical Shooting (App Lab) and Shotwood (App Lab), also indie tactical shooters, for ideas.

There was an update and now the controls are weird. The controls were fine for me before. I can turn/rotate but I cant move or I can move but not turn.

reviews.review_title: Good game but needs some additions
reviews.review_description:
First of all this game is well made and especially the live scenarios are great because they feel somehow much more dense and real than tactical assault for example regarding the cqc aspect.
Still some things should be polished up a bit:
First, the whole setting looks a bit trist. Grey in grey and few colors make it look a bit washy. The graphics are decent and to me this looks nicer than the Polygon-style of tactical assault for example.
It's good to have different training options from stationary targets up to a live scenario. The training house can also be randomised which is great.
Maybe some more weapons should be added which would greatly add to the replayability. But what's missing the most is some sort of hand to hand option when you run out of ammo.
So some sort of knife would be great or of this doesn't fit into the police/swat-context then you should be able to grab and redirect a weapon to buy yourself some time and punch an opponent down or be able to grab and push them down to ground and handcuff them similiar to what you do with the hostages. Like this you could also have the challenge to finish a mission without even killing anyone.
Enemies AI could also need some work. They should move faster and maybe seek some cover or run around a bit more but right now they seem to be a bit too static. 
Also some menu button ingame would be good to choose the destination where you want to go so you don't have to walk around the base and look for the type of scenario you want to play.
Some ingame option to quit your present mission would be good so you don't have to walk back to the swat truck all the time.
After your mission some sort of analysis would be good like how many targets you neutralised and how many hostages you saved, the time needed and maybe how many shots fired and how many hits you had.
Once the mission is over a clearer signal could be given or some menu could pop up to show you that you have just finished your job and don't have to walk back to your truck.
Lastly I don't get why you should plan your mission on a tacical table with several figures when you are on your own anyway. This would only make sense with multiplayer or if you had bots in your team that you could give orders to which would be a great add on as well.
So all in all I understand that this game is an Applab version and I really enjoy it. I also see a lot of potential in it but as it is right now it needs some more polishing.

The game is really cool, been looking for a game like this, and with future updates i see this game getting much bigger!

Some ideas - 
1. Shooting through doors
2. House maps, gas station, other CQB situations 
3. More guns
4. Breaching tools(c4, thermite, etc), snake cam
5. A hostage multiplayer mode
6. Co-op multiplayer against ai, hostage situation (team communication)
7. Shotguns (and chunks that break off of doors when you shoot them with shotguns or damage them enough)
8. 2 story buildings 

I know this game is new these are just ideas for future updates

developer_response.body: Happy to hear you are enjoying the game and thank you for the list of ideas.

reviews.review_description:
The game is amazing but Here’s some things you should add /1Co-op￼/2Frag grenades￼/2Realistic blood￼

￼

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you like the game and thank you for your suggestions.

reviews.review_description: Make sure you have controllers in perfect condition. I'll eat the $12 just to be able to say this was a decent concept with poor execution.

reviews.review_title: An amazing breaching and SWAT-style game
reviews.review_description:
This game is very well executed, I love the idea of being able to clear rooms, detaining hostages and getting rid of gunmen, and with the new addition of live scenarios, everything changed for the better.
I do have a couple suggestions/ideas for the developers:
1. Adding controller vibration when firing a gun is essential for immersion and would be a good addition.
2. A hijacked airplane type map, very good CQB with tight spaces but open angles so the player has to be extra cautious.
3. Generally more guns, HK416 for example.
4. IK arms just for more immersion and overall better looking compared to just floating hands. It can be a setting.

All-in-all, I highly suggest anyone who is into CQB and breaching game to try this out, very well worth it for the price.

I must say I absolutely love the premise of this game but when I logged in and start playing there was no option for smooth turn. So maybe I did it wrong or something but with no smooth turn this game is virtually unplayable for me. So hopefully there will be a smooth turn option in a later update but for the most part the game is pretty good.

developer_response.body:
There is a smooth turn option in the game, You can turn it on or off at any time by using the in game settings tablet in “Gameplay and VR options” it is the third setting down called “Turn Type” and there you can toggle if you want snap or smooth turn.
To get the settings tablet reach over your left shoulder and press the grab button and grab the tablet pen to click the buttons, if you need any more help the first video of the tutorial is showing how to get and use the tablet.
